prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `aaef0977`.
codex_response:
Implemented `aaef0977` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule is: start from the lone non-`7` seed on the all-`7` grid, then color every cell by its Manhattan distance from that seed using the fixed 9-color cycle `(3,4,0,5,2,8,9,6,1)`, phased so distance `0` is the seed’s own color. The Opus summary hint was used because it matched all official examples; no correction note was needed, and no new shared primitive was necessary.

Official verification passed `3/3`, the shared build produced `1000` verified examples, `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task aaef0977` passed `1000/1000`, and I visually checked the preview sheet. The generator stays on-family by keeping the orange background, exactly one seed cell, and square boards, while varying size slightly (`8..12`) so the builder can reach 1000 unique inputs.
